title: Superconducting Qubit Engineer - Quantum Hardware Company
setting: A hardware-focused quantum company building superconducting processors and supporting cryogenic infrastructure
companySize: Hardware team of 20-60 inside a company of 100-400 people
description: You work on a system where chip design, fabrication, microwave control, packaging, cryogenics, and calibration all interact. The role is half device engineering and half experimental discipline.

schedule.activity: Check device status, fridge conditions, and overnight calibration drift
schedule.note: Many mornings begin with asking why yesterday's fidelity moved.

schedule.activity: Review chip or package design changes with fabrication and control teams
schedule.note: Tiny physical changes can alter the whole control story.

schedule.activity: Run calibration routines and analyse qubit performance
schedule.note: Coherence, readout error, and crosstalk are always in view.

schedule.activity: Investigate one hardware bottleneck such as packaging loss or amplifier-chain noise
schedule.note: Progress often comes from removing one ugly experimental constraint at a time.

schedule.activity: Write up results for the device roadmap and next test cycle
schedule.note: The roadmap depends on honest interpretation, not only optimistic plots.

responsibilities: Coordinate across chip, package, cryogenic, and control teams
Feed hardware results back into the roadmap
challenges: Extreme sensitivity to noise and drift
Multi-layer problems where no single subsystem is clearly at fault
Balancing fast experimental iteration with reliable measurement practice
rewards: Working directly on one of the most advanced hardware stacks in the field
Seeing physics, engineering, and software converge in one place
Contributing to the part of quantum that is hardest to fake
careerPath: Quantum hardware engineer -> Senior qubit engineer -> Hardware lead -> Director of quantum hardware
